primary_accounting_issue: Qualification, au regard d’IFRS 9, des flux contractuels comme étant uniquement des remboursements de principal et d’intérêts (test SPPI) afin d’identifier les bases de classement et d’évaluation ultérieure d’un actif financier

authority_resolution.selection_reason: I applied the strict precedence rule that IFRS supersedes IAS where documents could overlap on the classification of financial instruments, and IFRS 9 directly governs the classification of financial assets based on business model and contractual cash flow characteristics, including the SPPI assessment.
authority_resolution.residual_uncertainty: low — IFRS 9 expressly addresses whether contractual cash flows are solely payments of principal and interest and links that assessment to the recognized measurement categories.

primary_authority.reason: These paragraphs directly govern the SPPI assessment and the resulting IFRS 9 classification and measurement categories for financial assets.

supporting_authority.reason: This definition helps distinguish debt-type financial assets from equity instruments, which matters because the SPPI-driven classification models in IFRS 9 apply to financial assets with relevant contractual cash flows rather than equity instruments.

peripheral_authority.reason: These paragraphs address receivables and contract assets from revenue contracts, not the peer IFRS 9 classification approaches arising from the SPPI issue.
These paragraphs relate to hedge accounting rather than the IFRS 9 SPPI classification issue.

treatment_families.family: Financial assets with SPPI cash flows held in a hold-to-collect business model
Financial assets with SPPI cash flows held in a business model achieved by both collecting contractual cash flows and selling
Financial assets failing the SPPI condition or otherwise not qualifying for the SPPI-based categories

approaches.label: Amortised cost
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies amortised cost as a top-level classification outcome when the asset has SPPI cash flows and is held in a hold-to-collect business model.

approaches.label: Fair value through other comprehensive income
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies fair value through other comprehensive income as a distinct top-level classification outcome when the asset has SPPI cash flows and is held in a collect-and-sell business model.

approaches.label: Fair value through profit or loss
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies fair value through profit or loss as the residual top-level classification outcome where the SPPI-based categories do not apply or the fair value option is used to eliminate or significantly reduce an accounting mismatch.
